name: Paris Blockchain Week 2026
location: Carrousel du Louvre, Paris, France (VIP dinner April 14 at Chateau de Versailles)
attendance: ~10,000 expected attendees from 100+ countries; 320+ speakers, 450+ journalists, 300 sponsors (2025 edition drew 9,500+ attendees and 420+ speakers) (forward-dated projection, not a realized figure; official sources vary as of 2026-06-18: live homepage cites 7,500+ attendees and 360+ journalists, other official listings cite 10,000 and 450+ journalists)
defi_themes: Stablecoins and stablecoin-powered payments (24/7 settlement, euro-indexed stablecoins, MiCA e-money tokens vs asset-referenced tokens)
Real World Asset (RWA) tokenization as institutional infrastructure (tokenized bonds, funds, fractional real estate)
Institutional DeFi / TradFi-DeFi convergence (banks integrating blockchain rails, closing the liquidity gap)
Tokenized assets and the global settlement layer (T+0 settlement, custody, clearing, execution at scale)
Tokenization in asset management and capital markets (natively tokenized IPO, equities in a token economy)
RWA liquidity and on-chain liquidity provision
Crypto/DeFi regulation in Europe (MiCA/MiCAR lessons, ESMA dialogue, EU vs US regulatory gap)
Bitcoin treasury strategies and on-chain digital credit/capital markets
Institutional adoption: ETPs, ETFs and stablecoins in institutional portfolios
Interoperability and account abstraction as rails for mass adoption
AI x DeFi (agentic on-chain wealth management, decentralized AI)

Equities approach in a token economy

notable_speakers.name: Emmanuel Macron
notable_speakers.affiliation: President of France (special address on stablecoins, digital euro, European financial sovereignty)

notable_speakers.name: Anne Le Henanff
notable_speakers.affiliation: French Minister-Delegate for AI and Digital Affairs

key_takeaways: Macron became the first sitting G7 head of state to deliver a keynote at a conference dedicated to blockchain/digital assets, framing euro-indexed stablecoins, the ECB digital euro, and European monetary sovereignty vs US/China as strategic priorities.

RWA tokenization was positioned as a 'trillion-dollar' institutional infrastructure layer, with the conversation shifting from proof-of-concept to operational deployment of tokenized bonds, funds and real estate by major asset managers (BlackRock, Amundi, Franklin Templeton, Fidelity).

Stablecoins were the dominant DeFi/payments theme: 24/7 stablecoin-powered settlement, the move toward T+0 (vs T+2), and MiCA's distinction between e-money tokens and asset-referenced tokens shaping European stablecoin issuance.

Heavy emphasis on institutional/TradFi-DeFi convergence: banks (Deutsche Bank, BNY, Citi, JPMorgan, BNP Paribas, Goldman, Morgan Stanley) and infrastructure providers (Circle, Fireblocks, Paxos, Canton Network) building the bridge between traditional finance and on-chain rails.

Regulation framed as the enabler of institutional DeFi: MiCA/MiCAR 'lessons learned', ESMA dialogue, and the EU-vs-US regulatory gap were recurring topics, with Europe presented as a relatively clear regime post-MiCAR.
Tokenized capital markets advanced beyond fixed income into equities, including discussion of the 'world's first natively tokenized IPO' (Lise) and equities in a token economy.
Bitcoin moved 'beyond store of value' into capital-markets territory: corporate Bitcoin treasury models and the emergence of on-chain Bitcoin-backed digital credit.
AI x DeFi emerged as a forward theme: agentic on-chain wealth management and decentralized AI infrastructure.

Overall positioning: 'Where Institutions and Digital Assets Finally Meet' - the event branded itself around the new institutional financial order built on tokenization, stablecoins and on-chain settlement.
